methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $85M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
Multiply Labs is a Series A-stage biotech and health company founded by Fred Parietti and Alice Melocchi that has raised $41M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a universe of 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers in Biotech & Health, including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, and Chai Discovery.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable triangulation method here. Using total capital raised ($41M+) as a baseline proxy yields an $85M reference point. Applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the next funding stage produces an implied valuation of $170M. This methodology reflects standard venture progression patterns for the sector. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (median 6.9x across the three public tickers) cannot be applied without the target's revenue, which remains undisclosed. Peer-median analysis is similarly inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting the narrow filter threshold.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $144M to $195M, with a median of $170M. This range reflects moderate confidence given reliance on a single applicable method rather than triangulation across multiple independent approaches. Key uncertainties include the absence of disclosed revenue (precluding public-comp anchoring), limited same-stage private peer data, and typical Series A stage opacity on unit economics and cash-burn profiles.
